description:
Attempted as integration test for fz-aiz arc. With ensure_any_keys +
annotation-driven dispatch, quicksort COMPILES cleanly (no .29.11
panics, no ABI mismatch). But execution hits a separate bug — possibly
list-of-int handling under deep recursion, or a specific Var ABI
mismatch in the recursive call's cont chain. Symptoms:

  - quicksort([])    prints "nil"     (correct — empty list)
  - quicksort([42])  prints "[42]"    (correct)
  - quicksort([7])   prints "[7]"     (correct — recursion on empty)
  - quicksort([2,1]) silent exit-0    (recursion on 1-element list breaks)
  - quicksort([5,4,3,2,1]) SIGSEGV

Closure-with-captures (`fn(x) -> x < p`) inside HOF also broken:

  map(fn(x) -> x + k, [1,2,3])  →  [false, #special<0x1b>, #special<0x23>]

So quicksort needs at least two more fixes outside the fz-aiz arc:
(1) recursive-call cont seam for list-of-int when concat/recursion
chain involves multiple specs; (2) closure-with-non-empty-captures
ABI at the call site.

Not part of fz-aiz — its bug class (.29.x panics, MakeClosure ABI
hazard, naturally_opaque vs any_key_specs conflation) IS fixed. apply2,
higher_order, curried_add, closure_typed_captures, actor_ring,
three_process_chain all green. quicksort exercises deeper closure /
recursive-cont machinery.

comments.text:
Root chased.

Symptom: each(fn(x) -> x+k, [1,2,3]) with k=10 prints 19, 27, 35
instead of 11, 12, 13. The wrong values decode as `(h*8+1) + k_raw`,
i.e. the caller passed h TAGGED and the callee added it as RAW.

Where: each_s17's TailCallClosure to the lambda lowers to:

  v18 = load.i64 v0+16
  return_call_indirect sig4, v18(v11, v0, v16)   ; v11 = list head, tagged

sig4 = (i64, i64, i64) -> i64 tail. That's the closure-target / indirect
sig — all-i64 with the convention that arg slots are Tagged. But the
callee `lambda_16_s22` has narrow ABI (RawInt, RawInt) -> Tagged. Wire
types match; semantic contract on slot 0 does not.

Why the indirect path: ir_codegen.rs:4994–5045 has a `lit_resolved`
fast-path that would emit a direct return_call with the body's narrow
sig. It's returning None for this case despite the dump showing
v0 :: &fn16[10]:(any) -> any (a singleton closure_lit).

Three candidate failure points inside lit_resolved (in order of
suspicion):
  1. resolve_tcc_body's `ft.vars.get(closure)?.as_closure_lit()?`
     returns None — Descr carries lit info in a form as_closure_lit
     doesn't recognize (union with another arrow, non-empty neg, etc).
  2. fn_ids.get(&body_sid) returns None — the resolved narrow spec
     didn't get an emitted Cranelift FuncId (sentinel or unreachable).
  3. closure_n_captures.get(&body_fn_id).unwrap_or(0) silently
     mis-routes when the entry is missing — n_caps=0 produces a wrong
     sig length but the path still falls through to indirect.

Bug pre-existed fz-aiz arc. Pre-arc, programs in this shape panicked
at .29.12.1 before reaching the wire-ABI mismatch. The arc removed
the panic; the underlying invariant break is now visible.

Two viable fixes (both outside fz-aiz):

A. Make lit_resolved actually fire. Trace each None-returning point
   and either fix the Descr / spec-registration upstream or relax the
   gating (e.g., as_closure_lit accepting wider shapes).

B. Make the indirect path coerce per-arg from caller-repr to callee-
   narrow-repr. The body's param_reprs are available — peek at them
   via body_func_id and coerce args before the indirect call.

(A) is the principled fix; (B) is a defense-in-depth seam.

title: quicksort fixture: deferred (post-arc bug surface)
